name: TDD-SUBAGENT-BUG-3
title: rabbit-feature-touch Step 4 does not read .rabbit-human-approval-bypass; dispatcher ignores the marker
description:
rabbit-feature-touch Step 4 (Human Approval) does not yet read the .rabbit-human-approval-bypass marker written by /rabbit-config human-approval bypass. The marker exists and sync-check.py alerts on it, but the dispatcher still waits for in-conversation approval regardless of whether the marker is present.

Expected: at Step 4, the dispatcher checks for .rabbit-human-approval-bypass at the repo root. If present, it passes --no-human-approval to dispatch-tdd-subagent.py (same as the current explicit bypass path) and emits a visible warning: "[rabbit] Step 4 skipped: human-approval bypass marker active. Run /rabbit-config human-approval gated to restore the gate."

Implementation: in the rabbit-feature-touch SKILL.md Step 4 section, replace the current bypass check (which only looks at an in-conversation flag) with a file-based check:

  1. Check if .rabbit-human-approval-bypass exists at repo root.
  2. If yes: log the warning, pass --no-human-approval to dispatch-tdd-subagent.py.
  3. If no: wait for in-conversation user approval as today.

This makes the bypass state fully durable and auditable — the marker file is the source of truth, not the conversation.

The marker itself is managed by /rabbit-config human-approval bypass|gated (PR #106). The dispatcher hookup is the missing consumer side.

history.note: TDD cycle complete. rabbit-feature-touch SKILL.md Step 4 now checks .rabbit-human-approval-bypass marker as FIRST action; emits named warning with revoke command; old in-conversation bypass language removed.
